title: Origin Fairness, Politeness, Retries, Backoff, and p-limit · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds publisher-respecting origin scheduler from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of publisher-respecting origin scheduler while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, ch…
objectives: Maintain per-origin concurrency, minimum spacing, next-eligible time, request and byte budgets, and owner overrides.
Retry only classified transient and idempotent cases with attempt, elapsed, and owner budgets.
Parse valid Retry-After, combine it with capped exponential backoff and bounded jitter, and record the chosen delay.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Origin Buckets
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 3085 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove per-origin eligibility, retry classification, Retry-After, bounded jitter, p-limit boundaries, and clock control; owner authorization and revocation must remain separate from robots and legal-review claims; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Origin Buckets.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for maintain per-origin concurrency, minimum spacing, next-eligible time, request and byte budgets, and owner overrides. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Origin Buckets, keep Origin Buckets connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.
